input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.base_prompt: Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

delta_p.trigger: When the current summary/output concludes that the provided passages do not link any artist to the album title mentioned in the question (e.g., current_output contains phrases like "Insufficient information" or "do not link any artist to an album titled").
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep all factual observations already extracted from the visible passages (which passages mention debut singles, and any songwriter attributions they contain). Do not remove or contradict any verified snippet that appears in the passages.
delta_p.change_rule: Instead of stopping at 'insufficient information', extend the summary to (1) enumerate which passages provide debut-song or songwriter information (list passage titles or short quoted snippets that appear in the provided passages), (2) explicitly state that none of those passages tie an artist to the album named in the question (use the album title exactly as in the question), (3) identify the missing bridge succinctly as the artist who released the album titled "I'm Still Here" (do not propose a name), and (4) give a compact, actionable retrieval direction for the next hop: fetch the artist/album page for the performer who released "I'm Still Here" and open that artist's debut-album / debut-song page (or the debut-song entry) to extract the songwriting credit(s). Provide these next-hop targets as generic page types (artist biography/album page, debut-song or debut-album tracklist page) rather than specific unknown names.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not assert or invent the artist's name or any songwriter not explicitly present in the visible passages. Do not copy or reproduce any gold/target-only labels or long literal spans that appear only in the target state. Avoid implying the answer has been found; avoid instructing the agent to output the final songwriter(s) here.
delta_p.output_contract: Produce the field `summary` that: (a) succinctly lists relevant evidence snippets or passage titles that mention debut singles and any songwriter attributions present in the visible passages, (b) states clearly that none of the provided passages connect an artist to the album titled "I'm Still Here", (c) exposes the missing bridge as 'the artist who released the album "I'm Still Here"' and (d) supplies a compact retrieval direction for the next hop: retrieve that artist's page and the artist's debut-album / debut-song page (or the debut-song entry) to extract the songwriter credit(s). Keep this output factual, concise, and actionable for the next retrieval agent.
delta_p.why_local_transition: This narrow prompt edit turns an unhelpful 'insufficient information' dead-end into a precise first-hop summary plus a concrete next-hop retrieval plan. It preserves all verified observations in the current passages while exposing the specific bridge (the performer of the album titled "I'm Still Here") and telling the next agent exactly which pages to fetch to obtain songwriting credits.

visible_agent_inputs.question: Who wrote the debut song on the debut album of the artist who also released the album "I'm Still Here"?
passages: Cold (But I'm Still Here) | "Cold (But I'm Still Here)" is the first single off Evans Blue's debut album, "The Melody and the Energetic Nature of Volume".  The song was released December 13, 2005, two months prior to the album release, and garnered frequent radio play in anticipation of the upcoming album.  Evans Blue's official MySpace explains the meaning of this song as well as others by the group.  A music video was also produced for "Cold (But I'm Still Here)."  The song currently has over three million plays on MySpace.

Kick Back Relax | "Kick Back Relax" is a pop song recorded by Swedish singer and Idol winner Agnes taken from her second album "Stronger".  The track was written by Jörgen Elofsson who also wrote her debut single "Right Here Right Now".  It was released as the album's first single in September 2006.

Crazy Over You | "Crazy Over You" is a debut song recorded by American country music duo Foster & Lloyd, who also wrote the song.  It was released in May 1987 as the first single from their self-titled debut album.  It was their most successful single, peaking at #4 on the "Billboard" Hot Country Songs chart in 1987.  The song was simultaneously recorded and released by Ricky Van Shelton on his debut album, "Wild-Eyed Dream".

Outta Here (song) | "Outta Here" is the title song of the debut studio album by Dutch R&B singer-songwriter Esmée Denters.  It was released as the debut single both from the artist and the album in the Netherlands on 14 April 2009, 27 April in New Zealand followed by 16 August in the UK.

Gabriela (singer) | Her self-titled debut album was released in 2001; it was produced by Vehnee Saturno, who also wrote eight of the album's ten songs.  Another of the songs was a rendition of Bryan Adams's "Please Forgive Me".  Her second album, "In the Interim", was released in 2004.

Anywhere but Here (song) | "Anywhere but Here" is the title of the fourth single released by American country music artist Sammy Kershaw.  It was co-written by Bob DiPiero and John Scott Sherrill with Buddy Cannon, who also produced it.  It was released in September 1992 as the final single from his debut album, "Don't Go Near the Water".  It peaked at #10 on the "Billboard" Hot Country Songs chart and #17 on the Canadian "RPM" country singles chart.

Dollhouse (Melanie Martinez song) | "Dollhouse" is the debut single by American recording artist Melanie Martinez.  The song was featured on Martinez's debut EP "Dollhouse" (2014), where it was released as the EP's lead single on February 9, 2014.  "Dollhouse" was later featured on Martinez's debut studio album, "Cry Baby" (2015).  Lyrically, "Dollhouse" is about a dysfunctional family, who, according to Martinez, is "hiding being a perfect plastic facade".  Martinez has also stated that "Dollhouse" is a metaphor for how people view celebrities and their seemingly perfect public lives.  Dollhouse is also the second song of Cry Baby, and this song is also the first official song Melanie Martinez wrote.
visible_agent_inputs.current_output: Insufficient information in the provided passages to answer. None of the passages link any artist to an album titled "I'm Still Here," so the writer of that artist's debut-song cannot be determined from the given text.

input_dict.output_requirement: Return one summary_1 string. Summarize the visible hop1 passages and expose a useful second-hop retrieval direction.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are executing one agent in a fixed HotpotQA multi-agent pipeline.

Use the unchanged base instruction p together with the complete ORDERED
list of local prompt deltas. The deltas are supplemental behavior guidance
for this sample; they are not outputs and must not be quoted mechanically.

This is a direct delta-context execution probe:
- Do not rewrite, aggregate, or synthesize a new prompt p'.
- Apply every delta in order to the visible inputs.
- Judge success only by producing the endpoint agent output.
- Do not attempt to reproduce or discuss intermediate states.
- Do not infer hidden information from the existence of the deltas.
- Use only the visible agent inputs as factual evidence.
- Never output gold/oracle labels, evaluation metrics, or hidden support.
- Preserve the agent's normal input/output contract.

response:
{
  "output": "Relevant evidence in the provided passages (debut-song mentions and songwriter attributions):
1) "Cold (But I'm Still Here)" — "\"Cold (But I'm Still Here)\" is the first single off Evans Blue's debut album, \"The Melody and the Energetic Nature of Volume\"." (identifies a debut single for Evans Blue; no songwriter given)
2) "Kick Back Relax" — "The track was written by Jörgen Elofsson who also wrote her debut single \"Right Here Right Now\"." (songwriter attribution for the artist's debut single)
3) "Crazy Over You" — "\"Crazy Over You\" is a debut song... Foster & Lloyd, who also wrote the song." (the duo are credited as writers of their debut song)
4) "Outta Here (song)" — "\"Outta Here\" is the title song of the debut studio album... released as the debut single both from the artist and the album." (marks a debut single)
5) "Gabriela (singer)" — "Her self-titled debut album... produced by Vehnee Saturno, who also wrote eight of the album's ten songs." (songwriter credits for most tracks on the debut album)
6) "Anywhere but Here (song)" — "It was released... as the final single from his debut album, \"Don't Go Near the Water\". It was co-written by Bob DiPiero and John Scott Sherrill with Buddy Cannon." (co-writer credits for that single)
7) "Dollhouse (Melanie Martinez song)" — "\"Dollhouse\" is the debut single... this song is also the first official song Melanie Martinez wrote." (artist credited as writer of her debut single)

None of these passages connect any artist to an album titled "I'm Still Here". The missing bridge is: the artist who released the album "I'm Still Here". Next-hop retrieval direction (compact and actionable): fetch the performer/artist biography page (or the album page) for the artist who released "I'm Still Here", then open that artist's debut-album page or the debut-song entry (or the debut-album tracklist) to extract the songwriting credit(s) for the debut song.",
  "rationale": "Preserved all factual snippets mentioning debut songs and songwriter attributions from the visible passages, explicitly stated that none link to the album "I'm Still Here", exposed the missing bridge as the artist who released that album, and provided concrete generic page-level retrieval targets for the next hop as required by the ordered prompt edits."
